meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THIS PLAN UP TO DATE AFTER EVERY SUB-STEP. LOG EVERY DEVIATION, DISCOVERY, AND REPLAN INSIDE THIS FILE BEFORE PROCEEDING. IF REALITY CHANGES, RE-READ THE RELEVANT CODE, THEN REWRITE THE AFFECTED TASKS IMMEDIATELY.
meta.completionPolicy: NO ESTIMATES. NO PRIORITIES. NO SEPARATE TESTING TASKS. NO SEPARATE COMMIT TASKS. EVERY TASK MUST INCLUDE ITS OWN REPRODUCTION, VALIDATION, STRICT-REVIEW-GATE PASSES, AND A SINGLE SELF-CONTAINED COMMIT BEFORE THE NEXT TASK BEGINS. WHEN THIS LIST IS EXHAUSTED, EXPAND IT AGAIN FROM EVIDENCE, NOT WISHFUL THINKING.

globalAlerts.warning: MANDATORY PER-TASK COMMIT PROTOCOL: BEFORE MARKING ANY TASK DONE OR MAKING ANY COMMIT, REREAD `strict-review-gate` SKILL IN FULL, RUN THE RULE OF TWO UNTIL TWO CONTIGUOUS ISSUE-FREE REVIEWS EXAMINE THE EXACT SAME DIFF, REREAD `git-commit` SKILL IN FULL, COMMIT ONLY THAT ONE TASK'S DIFF, THEN REREAD `DIRECTIVE.txt` BEFORE STARTING THE NEXT TASK. DO NOT BATCH MULTIPLE TASKS INTO ONE COMMIT.

THE NINE-HOUR SESSION TIMER IS MANDATORY. THE `session-timer-start` / `session-timer-check` TARGETS WERE EXECUTED ON 2026-04-14 (ON MACOS THE SHELL FORM IS `gmake ...`), BUT `config.mk` CURRENTLY HARD-CODES `scratch/session_timer_20260413.txt`. TREAT THAT STALE FILENAME AS A REAL DEFECT AND FIX IT IN TASK 1 BEFORE TRUSTING ANY FUTURE TIMER OUTPUT.

THE OLD BLUEPRINT WAS STALE: IT WAS DOMINATED BY COMPLETED BUBBLETEA MIGRATION WORK, REFERENCED A MISSING `scratch/termmux-architecture.md`, AND CONTAINED STUB TASKS THAT DO NOT MATCH THE CURRENT CODEBASE. DO NOT TRUST OLD PLAN ARTIFACTS OVER FIRST-HAND CODE READING.

`osm pr-split` IS NOT PRIME-TIME UNTIL A USER CAN FOCUS THE CLAUDE PANE, TYPE DIRECTLY, USE MOUSE INPUT, ENTER AND EXIT CTRL+] PASSTHROUGH, RETURN TO THE SAME WIZARD CONTEXT, AND RESUME WORK WITHOUT LOSING SESSION STATE OR VERIFY CONTEXT.

CLAUDE AND VERIFY MUST CONVERGE ON A SINGLE SESSIONMANAGER-BASED MENTAL MODEL. `getClaudePaneSession()` IN `pr_split_13_tui.js` IS CURRENTLY A SPECIAL PROXY OVER `tuiMux.session()/writeToChild()/childScreen()/screenshot`, WHILE VERIFY USES `_buildVerifyProxy()` OVER `tuiMux.register()/snapshot()/input()/switchTo()`. THIS ASYMMETRY IS A CORE QUALITY PROBLEM.

PUBLIC TEXT IS NOT YET TRUSTWORTHY. `CHANGELOG.md` AND `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md` CURRENTLY OVERSTATE OR LAG REALITY (INCLUDING SHELL-PANE REFERENCES AND INTERACTIVITY CLAIMS). DO NOT TREAT DOCUMENTATION AS EVIDENCE UNTIL TASK 11 RECONCILES IT WITH CODE AND TRUNK DIFFS.

ALL PROJECT CHECKS MUST PASS ON MACOS, LINUX, AND WINDOWS. ON MACOS USE GNU MAKE (`gmake`). FOR INTERACTIVE PR-SPLIT / TERMMUX WORK, `test-prsplit-fast`, `test-prsplit-e2e`, `test-termmux`, `make-all-in-container`, AND `make-all-run-windows` ARE THE MINIMUM SIGNAL PATHS.
NO SHIMS, NO COEXISTING PATHS, NO DEAD SPECIAL-CASE WRAPPERS. WHEN A SESSIONMANAGER-BASED PATH REPLACES THE AD-HOC CLAUDE PROXY, DELETE THE OLD BRANCHES AND UPDATE EVERY CALL SITE AND TEST.

statusSection.currentState: Blueprint rewritten on 2026-04-14 after rereading DIRECTIVE.txt, blueprint-json / strict-review-gate / git-commit skills, config.mk, `pr_split` chunks, `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`, `internal/termmux/manager.go`, `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and observation tests. The real frontier is `osm pr-split`: strategy quality vs trunk is under-proven, Claude still uses `getClaudePaneSession()` instead of a true SessionManager proxy, direct pane interaction is not proven end-to-end, persistence/resume is only partially truthful, and docs/changelog are ahead of reality. This checkout resolves trunk through `origin/main`, not a local `main` branch, so future audits must resolve `trunkRef` instead of guessing. The session timer targets were executed at 2026-04-14 06:31:35 and checked with `Elapsed: 0m | Remaining: 540m`, but the hard-coded timer filename in `config.mk` is stale and is now Task 1.
statusSection.goalState: `osm pr-split` is ready for prime time against trunk: representative trunk-like diffs prove strategy selection and split-plan quality; Claude and verify panes both run through SessionManager-backed interactive sessions; keyboard, mouse, passthrough, lifecycle, and resume behavior are proven end-to-end on macOS, Linux, and Windows; public docs/changelog tell the truth; and every task has been completed through the mandatory per-task Rule-of-Two review and single-task commit protocol.
replanLog: 2026-04-14: Full blueprint rewrite. Removed stale migration-era focus, missing scratch-doc references, and misleading stub tasks. Re-centered the plan on the current pr-split / termmux production gap after reading `pr_split_09_claude.js`, `pr_split_13_tui.js`, `pr_split_16c/16d/16e/16f/16g`, `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`, `internal/termmux/manager.go`, `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the observation / inline PTY tests.

2026-04-14: Session timer mandate executed through the configured Make targets: start time 2026-04-14 06:31:35, projected end time 2026-04-14 15:31:35, and check output `Elapsed: 0m | Remaining: 540m`. The hard-coded `scratch/session_timer_20260413.txt` filename in `config.mk` is now treated as a concrete defect, not a mere note.

mandatoryDirectives.perTaskCommitProtocol: MANDATORY FOR EVERY TASK, WITHOUT EXCEPTION: (1) reread `.claude/skills/strict-review-gate/SKILL.md` in full; (2) execute the Rule of Two until two contiguous issue-free reviews inspect the exact same diff; if any code changes after a review, reset the counter to zero; (3) reread `.claude/skills/git-commit/SKILL.md` in full; (4) commit only that task's diff immediately; (5) reread `DIRECTIVE.txt` before touching the next task. Testing and verification are part of the task itself, never their own tasks.
mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: At session start and before any replan, reread `blueprint.json`, `WIP.md`, and `DIRECTIVE.txt`. Before each task, open the specific files named in the task description instead of relying on memory or old notes. If a task uncovers reality that contradicts this blueprint, update the blueprint before writing more code.
mandatoryDirectives.timerMandate: The nine-hour punishment timer is real. Until Task 1 repairs the stale target, keep recording the current timer file path, start epoch, and shell-check output in `WIP.md`. Every resume must prove elapsed and remaining time from the same file via shell-based `date` math, not hand-waved memory.
mandatoryDirectives.trunkTruthMandate: Before any trunk audit, resolve `trunkRef` and write it into `WIP.md`. Use this order: `git symbolic-ref --quiet --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, then fall back to `origin/main`, `origin/master`, `main`, `master`. Once resolved, use `git diff <trunkRef>...HEAD` and `git log --oneline <trunkRef>..HEAD` consistently. The branch is not ready until the diff vs that real trunk ref is explicable file-by-file and the remaining gaps are written down instead of implied.
mandatoryDirectives.tddMandate: For behavior changes and bug fixes, write or update the focused reproducer first, prove the old behavior is missing or broken, then fix it and rerun the focused reproducer plus the broader package/Make targets. For strategy quality work, fixture-based acceptance must describe what plan behavior becomes trustworthy, not just that tests are green.
mandatoryDirectives.sessionManagerMandate: Prefer SessionManager-backed sessions, `tuiMux.register`, `tuiMux.snapshot`, `tuiMux.input`, `tuiMux.activate`, and `tuiMux.switchTo`. Do not preserve the special Claude proxy once a real registered Claude session exists. Unify abstractions and delete the fallback paths.
mandatoryDirectives.documentationTruthfulness: No doc, README, or changelog text may claim an interaction mode, shell tab, test count, or prime-time behavior that the code and black-box tests cannot prove. Documentation repair is not cosmetic here; it is part of restoring trustworthy engineering reality.
mandatoryDirectives.scopeExpansion: After Task 12, do not stop. Convert the resulting evidence into the next frontier tasks immediately so the blueprint never empties. New work must come from remaining trunk gaps, reusable termmux opportunities, or production-risk evidence, not from easy filler.

sequentialTasks.task: Repair session timer and execution scaffolding
sequentialTasks.description: Update `config.mk` so `session-timer-start` and `session-timer-check` stop hard-coding `scratch/session_timer_20260413.txt` and instead read/write a stable current-session timer file that survives restarts. Record the chosen file path, start epoch, projected end time, and latest check output in a real `WIP.md`. Keep the shell math portable by using the existing macOS/Linux `date -v+9H` / `date -d '+9 hours'` pattern, and make the task instructions explicitly tell the next executor how to prove the nine-hour window before doing any work. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A cold-start executor can resume the repo, run the timer commands, inspect `WIP.md`, and know exactly whether the nine-hour window is still active without guessing which file matters. The timer filename is no longer stale or date-specific, the same file is used for both start and check, and the resulting diff is narrow enough to be reviewed and committed alone under the mandatory per-task protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Build trunk gap evidence pack and replan checkpoint
sequentialTasks.description: Create `scratch/pr-split-trunk-gap-audit.md` from first-hand evidence after first resolving and recording `trunkRef` in `WIP.md` (this checkout currently points at `origin/main`, not a local `main`). Use `git diff <trunkRef>...HEAD`, `git log --oneline <trunkRef>..HEAD`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md`, `internal/command/pr_split_09_claude.js`, `pr_split_13_tui.js`, `pr_split_16d_tui_handlers_claude.js`, `pr_split_16e_tui_update.js`, `pr_split_16f_tui_model.js`, `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`, and `internal/termmux/manager.go`. The doc must explicitly record the current user-visible blockers for direct Claude-pane interaction, the strategy-quality risks vs trunk, and every stale or misleading public claim discovered. Update `statusSection.replanLog` before moving on if this audit changes scope. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The repository contains a single authoritative evidence pack that tells a new executor what changed versus trunk, what `pr-split` publicly claims, what the Claude and verify panes actually do today, and which blockers are proven by reproduction rather than speculation. The plan can move to strategy and interactivity work without repeating discovery, and the audit diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Prove strategy selection and split-plan quality on trunk-like diffs
sequentialTasks.description: Turn the trunk audit into fixture-backed tests under `internal/command/` and `testdata/` for `prSplit.analyzeDiffAsync` in `pr_split_01_analysis.js`, `selectStrategy` / `selectStrategyAsync` / `applyStrategy` / `applyStrategyAsync` in `pr_split_02_grouping.js`, plan creation in `pr_split_03_planning.js`, and orchestrator entry points in `pr_split_10d_pipeline_orchestrator.js` and `pr_split_16b_tui_handlers_pipeline.js`. Cover representative trunk-like cases: multi-directory Go changes, generated-file churn, rename-heavy diffs, mixed docs+code updates, and dependency-shaped changes that should or should not cohabit a split. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: On curated diffs derived from real trunk-style work, `osm pr-split` produces stable, reviewable plans whose chosen strategy is explainable from the fixture output rather than by hand-waving. Generated-file noise, risky mixed changes, and dependency-heavy edits are surfaced or separated in a way a reviewer can trust, and the strategy-quality diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Prove Claude-pane keyboard and mouse routing end to end
sequentialTasks.description: Expand `pr_split_inline_e2e_test.go`, `pr_split_16_input_routing_test.go`, `pr_split_16_split_mouse_test.go`, and add any focused `pr_split_16_*` tests needed to cover `keyToTermBytes`, `CLAUDE_RESERVED_KEYS`, `INTERACTIVE_RESERVED_KEYS`, `handleKeyMessage`, `handleMouseMessage`, `computeSplitPaneContentOffset`, and `writeMouseToPane` across `pr_split_16d_tui_handlers_claude.js` and `pr_split_16e_tui_update.js`. Prove focused Claude-pane behavior for printable keys, Enter, Ctrl combos, reserved pane-management keys, mouse click/release/motion/wheel, and split-view focus changes. Use at least one Go-side SessionManager-backed recorder so bytes are proven to reach the PTY path, not just mocked JS helpers. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A user can be shown concrete tests proving that focusing the Claude pane sends intended keys and mouse gestures to the child session, while reserved navigation keys still belong to the wizard when they should. Failures localize to a specific routing layer instead of leaving 'direct pane interaction is broken' as an anecdote, and the routing-proof diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Replace Claude special proxy with SessionManager-backed session plumbing
sequentialTasks.description: Refactor `ClaudeCodeExecutor.spawn` in `pr_split_09_claude.js`, `getClaudePaneSession` in `pr_split_13_tui.js`, the split-view state in `pr_split_16f_tui_model.js`, and any necessary Go glue in `pr_split.go` / `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go` so Claude is registered with `tuiMux.register()` and addressed through an explicit SessionManager session ID plus a real proxy builder, not through the current ad hoc `{write, screen, output}` wrapper over `tuiMux.session()/writeToChild()/childScreen()/screenshot`. Delete the superseded special-case branches once the new path is wired everywhere. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Claude and verify now use one coherent session abstraction: Claude output comes from SessionManager snapshots, Claude input and resize target a concrete registered session, active-session changes are explicit, and the codebase no longer has a split-brain model where verify is managed and Claude is a special exception. The user-visible result is a Claude pane that behaves like a real muxed session, and the unification diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Deliver true Claude passthrough and return-to-wizard behavior
sequentialTasks.description: After Claude is SessionManager-backed, update `_onToggle` in `pr_split_16f_tui_model.js`, `handleToggleReturn` in `pr_split_16e_tui_update.js`, and any required termmux wrapper behavior in `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go` so Ctrl+] enters passthrough for the actually focused interactive pane, preserves the previously active session, and restores split-view focus, tab, notifications, and verify context when control returns. Expand `pr_split_16_ctrl_bracket_test.go`, `pr_split_16_keyboard_crash_test.go`, `pr_split_16_focus_nav_edge_test.go`, and `pr_split_termmux_observation_test.go` to prove the behavior with real PTY observation, not just guard checks. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: In a real observation test, a user can focus Claude, press Ctrl+], interact directly with the Claude terminal, press Ctrl+] again, and land back in the same wizard context without losing split-view state or verify progress. When no interactive session exists, the user receives a truthful notification instead of silent failure, and the passthrough diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Harden Claude lifecycle output and backpressure handling
sequentialTasks.description: Reduce blind dependence on `pollClaudeScreenshot` in `pr_split_16d_tui_handlers_claude.js` by wiring `tuiMux` event callbacks from `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go` (`output`, `bell`, `exit`, `closed`, `activated`) into Claude-pane status, crash/exit handling, and question detection. Add bounded write buffering or explicit retry/error propagation so temporary PTY failures do not silently drop user input. Audit `pr_split_14b_tui_commands_ext.js`, `pr_split_10b_pipeline_send.js`, and any focus/notification state that currently assumes passive polling. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Long-running Claude sessions update promptly enough for a user to trust the pane, bell/exit/crash conditions are visible instead of mysterious, and transient write failures no longer eat typed input without explanation. The user can tell the difference between idle, waiting-for-input, crashed, and closed, and the reliability diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Make persistence and resume truthful for Claude and verify sessions
sequentialTasks.description: Extend the existing mux persistence path in `pr_split_16g_persistence.js`, `pr_split.go`, `internal/builtin/termmux/module.go`, and `internal/termmux/persistence.go` so restart/resume behavior tells the truth about both Claude and verify sessions. Capture enough metadata to distinguish live sessions, dead PIDs, stale worktrees, and unrecoverable state, then surface that truth in the TUI and cleanup paths. Expand existing recovery tests such as `pr_split_autosplit_recovery_test.go` and any binary / observation tests needed to cover abnormal exit and clean exit paths. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: If `osm pr-split` dies mid-session, restarting produces an honest resume story: live Claude/verify sessions can be reattached, dead sessions are clearly marked dead and cleaned up safely, and stale state files do not resurrect fake work. Resume is proven by integration behavior, not merely by the existence of a JSON file, and the persistence diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Extract a reusable JS termmux facade and demonstration script
sequentialTasks.description: Turn the session-management patterns now embedded in `pr_split` into a reusable JS-facing facade over `osm:termmux`: register, activate, snapshot, input, resize, passthrough, event subscription, and persistence. Keep the reusable pieces outside the app-specific wizard logic, update `pr_split` to consume the same facade instead of bespoke helpers where practical, and add a demonstration script plus supporting docs under `scripts/` and `docs/reference/` showing a multi-pane interactive flow built directly on termmux. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Another command or script author can build a multi-pane interactive experience without copying `pr-split` internals, and `pr-split` itself relies on the same documented facade for the shared mux semantics. This proves `internal/termmux` is a real reusable subsystem rather than a one-off sidecar, and the facade diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Harden interactive behavior across macOS Linux and Windows
sequentialTasks.description: Add or refine focused Make targets in `config.mk` for high-signal interactivity checks (for example, targeted `pr-split` interactive, resume, and termmux passthrough runs), then fix the platform-specific PTY, resize, signal, shell, or fallback issues they expose across macOS, Linux-in-container, and Windows. This task must explicitly cover the SessionManager / passthrough paths touched in tasks 4-8 and use `hack/run-on-windows.sh`, `make-all-in-container`, and the focused `test-prsplit-*` / `test-termmux` targets rather than only local ad hoc commands. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A contributor can run a small, explicit command set and prove that direct Claude input, verify interactivity, passthrough, resize, and resume behavior are consistent on all supported platforms or are clearly documented where a platform-specific fallback is intentional. Prime-time claims are no longer macOS-only folklore, and the cross-platform hardening diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Reconcile README architecture docs and changelog with reality
sequentialTasks.description: Audit and update `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md`, `docs/reference/command.md`, and any other touched docs so they stop overstating Claude interactivity, stop referring to removed shell-pane concepts, and accurately describe current strategy behavior, split-view tabs, passthrough semantics, resume behavior, and test coverage. Use the evidence pack and the finished interactive tests as the source of truth. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A reader following the public docs gets an accurate picture of what `pr-split` can do today: how strategy selection behaves, how the Claude and verify panes differ or no longer differ, which keys manage the panes, how resume works, and what remains intentionally constrained. No document claims a shell tab, test scenario, or prime-time interaction path that the code cannot actually deliver, and the documentation diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

sequentialTasks.task: Run final trunk truth audit and repopulate the next frontier
sequentialTasks.description: After tasks 1-11 are individually reviewed and committed, rerun the full branch-vs-trunk audit using the resolved `trunkRef` recorded in `WIP.md`, targeted and full Make checks, and a hostile reread of the evidence pack, docs, and current code. Write `scratch/pr-split-final-audit.md` summarizing what is now actually true, what still blocks 'prime time' if anything, and which high-impact next-frontier tasks follow from the evidence. Update `blueprint.json` before stopping so the task list never goes empty. Keep this as one self-contained diff that can pass the mandatory per-task commit protocol by itself.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The branch is explainable against trunk file-by-file, the current reality of `pr-split` is written down without spin, and `blueprint.json` is immediately repopulated with new high-impact work instead of being declared finished. The audit itself becomes a trustworthy handoff artifact, and the expansion diff is reviewable and committed alone under the mandatory protocol.

perTask: Before changing code for a task, run or create the focused reproducer / fixture that proves the current gap. After the change, rerun that reproducer plus the relevant package tests for every touched file.

For `internal/command/pr_split_*` changes, run `gmake test-prsplit-fast` plus the smallest precise `go test -run ... ./internal/command/...` slices that exercise the touched handlers, screens, or orchestrator paths.
For `internal/termmux/` or `internal/builtin/termmux/` changes, run `gmake test-termmux` and the precise `go test -run ...` slices that cover the session wrapper or persistence path you changed.

For passthrough, direct pane interaction, persistence, or observation work, run `gmake test-prsplit-e2e` and any focused observation / binary tests needed to prove user-visible behavior, not just unit tests.
crossPlatform: macOS native: use GNU Make (`gmake`) for the focused and full checks.
Linux: use `gmake make-all-in-container` once a task's focused checks are green; do not defer Linux until the very end for PTY/session work.
Windows: use `gmake make-all-run-windows` after any SessionManager, PTY, signal, or persistence change; if behavior differs, fix it or document the intentional fallback in the same task.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If any reproducer, unit test, integration test, platform check, or review pass fails, the task remains In Progress. Fix the diff or replan before touching the next task.
continuousVerification.reviewPolicy: No task moves to Done until two contiguous strict-review-gate subagent reviews PASS on the identical diff and that exact task diff is committed by itself.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: DO NOT MARK A TASK DONE JUST BECAUSE CODE EXISTS. EACH TASK MUST DELIVER ITS USER-VISIBLE OR STRATEGY-VISIBLE EFFECT, SURVIVE THE RULE OF TWO, AND BE COMMITTED ALONE.
IF THE TIMER FILE CANNOT BE FOUND OR VERIFIED, ASSUME THE NINE-HOUR WINDOW HAS JUST RESTARTED AND RECORD IT AGAIN BEFORE PROCEEDING.
IF CLAUDE STILL RELIES ON `getClaudePaneSession()` SPECIAL-CASE BEHAVIOR OR DOCS STILL CLAIM A REMOVED SHELL TAB, THE PLAN IS NOT COMPLETE.
AFTER THE FINAL AUDIT, EXPAND THE BLUEPRINT AGAIN. AN EMPTY TASK LIST IS A FAILURE OF IMAGINATION AND OF EVIDENCE COLLECTION.
